summary: Focused testing of Event Delete feature (DELETE /api/events/{id} + frontend delete UI on Event Detail). Backend pytest suite (5/5 PASSED): (1) owner delete returns 200 + {detail} and subsequent GET returns 404 and event is absent from list; (2) DELETE without Authorization header returns 401; (3) DELETE with a different user's token returns 404 while the original event still exists for the owner (isolation preserved); (4) DELETE for a random non-existent uuid returns 404; (5) DELETE with a malformed bearer token returns 401. Frontend mobile-viewport (390x844) full acceptance path PASSED: signed in as test@hymn.app, created a new event 'DeleteMe-<ts>', opened it from Timeline, tapped event-detail-delete-button (trash), delete-confirm-modal appeared, delete-cancel-button closed modal (event still on Timeline), reopened detail, delete-confirm-button issued the DELETE and router.replace navigated to /timeline where the event is no longer visible; after browser reload it is still gone; after logout via logout-button and re-login with same credentials the event is still gone (visible-text count = 0 across all three assertions). No react-native or design blockers introduced by this feature.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warnings 'shadow*' style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow on web, and props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Non-blocking, pre-existing (not introduced by delete feature). Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx

critical_code_review_comments: server.py delete_event uses a single delete_one({id, user_id}) so ownership + non-existence both collapse to deleted_count==0 -> 404. This correctly conflates 'not yours' with 'not found', preventing user-enumeration attacks — the spec explicitly asks for 404 in the cross-user case, matched.

Frontend event/[id].tsx: modal is a proper RN Modal with transparent backdrop; disabled={deleting} on both Cancel and Confirm avoids double-submit; onRequestClose is guarded so the modal cannot be dismissed mid-delete. Uses router.replace('/(tabs)/timeline') so the detail screen is removed from history — user cannot go back into a deleted event.
Timeline re-fetches via useFocusEffect (confirmed via visual verification), so the deleted event disappears without an explicit reload.
api.deleteEvent hits DELETE /events/{id} with auth:true — bearer header attached from tokenStorage, verified end-to-end.
seed_data_creation: Created one ephemeral TEST_delete_other_<ts>@hymn.app user (via /api/auth/signup) plus multiple ephemeral events under test@hymn.app; all events either deleted by the test itself or via best-effort cleanup in finally-blocks. No persistent seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Delete testIDs: event-detail-delete-button, delete-confirm-modal, delete-cancel-button, delete-confirm-button. On web the router.replace target /(tabs)/timeline resolves to URL path /timeline. Text visibility on RN Web should be checked via computed style walk (see /app/backend/tests/test_event_delete.py sibling frontend script) rather than raw locator.count(), because a just-unmounted detail screen can briefly leave title text in the DOM tree.
rca_of_issue: No bugs found. Feature works end-to-end.
